arts.ttl: Who is Rio Ngumoha? Arne Slot hands Liverpool debut to 16-year-old wonderkid against Accrington Stanley in the FA Cup
arts.sum: The Reds face Accrington Stanley in the FA Cup third round this afternoon. Here is everything you need to know about Liverpool's 16-year-old wonderkid Rio Ngumoha.

arts.ttl: Rodrigo Bentancur pictured back home as he poses in touching family photo two days on from worrying on-pitch collapse during Tottenham's win over Liverpool
arts.sum: Bentancur fell to the ground unchallenged during the first half of the Carabao Cup semi-final tie at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium and sustained concussion.

arts.ttl: Rio Ngumoha makes Liverpool debut as Arne Slot makes eight changes for Accrington Stanley
arts.sum: Liverpool team news confirmed from Anfield as Arne Slot makes eight changes in total for the visit of Accrington Stanley in the third round of the FA Cup

arts.ttl: Liverpool v Accrington Stanley: FA Cup third round – live
arts.sum: FA Cup news from the 12.15pm GMT kick-off at Anfield Live scoreboard | Subscribe to Football Daily | Email Scott Stanley captain Shaun Whalley is asked by ITV’s Gabriel Clarke about the possibility of scoring a goal at the Kop end. A broad smile immediately plays across his face. “Yeah I’d love that,” Whalley replies. Then, with a twinkle in the eye, he adds: “I probably wouldn’t celebrate to be honest. I’ve got too many mates in there!” A laugh as he walks off, dreaming of all possibilities. The magic of the cup, right there. Accrington Stanley manager John Doolan speaks to ITV. “Just excitement … you can see the drive in them … it’s a fantastic day … we just want to get going … it’s special, isn’t it … the lads who got us three points last week get the shirt … you can’t go on sentiment … [former Liverpool academy player Liam Coyle] is a fantastic player and I’m sure he’ll get on the pitch at some point … you’ve got to do a professional job … Liverpool are a fantastic team … we’ve got to try to impose our game on them … look after the ball a bit better … match them and see where that takes us … we have a game-plan … we’ll do our best.” Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Liverpool's FA Cup clash with Accrington Stanley has kick-off time moved - Here's why
arts.sum: Liverpool take on Accrington Stanley in the FA Cup third round on Saturday and the game at Anfield has an earlier than usual 12.15pm kick-off time

arts.ttl: Liverpool vs Accrington: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: Arne Slot will receive his first taste of the FA Cup's magic on Saturday lunchtime when Liverpool host Accrington Stanley in the third round. A similar fate is unlikely to befall Slot's side this weekend, with Accrington currently languishing in 19th in League Two. The teams have only clashed once before, an FA Cup duel dating back to 1956.

arts.ttl: Liverpool vs Accrington Stanley LIVE: FA Cup team news and line-ups as minnows take on Premier League leaders
arts.sum: The Premier League leaders take on the fourth-tier side in the third round of the competition
